title: Episode 260625/ 				  25 Jun 2026
content:
# BBC Learning English 今日阅读

## 文章标题
Episode 260625/ Are you a fussy eater?

## 原文内容
Are you a fussy eater? Many of us remember foods that we used to hate as children – some of us still do hate those foods! But what can parents do to help encourage their children to eat a healthy diet, without turning every dinner into disaster? Neil and Georgie discuss this and teach you some new vocabulary.

Which of the following does the child nutritionist Charlotte Stirling-Reed recommend for stress-free family meals?

a) make sure your child eats five pieces of fruit and veg a day
b) don't pressure your child to eat everything on the plate
c) introduce your child to lots of new flavours quickly

Listen to the programme to hear the answer.

ups and downs
a mixture of good and bad things happening to someone

mood
how a person is feeling at a particular time

battleground
place where argument or conflict happens

a big deal
something of special importance

food allergy
medical condition where certain foods make your body sick

winning formula
(idiom) a method or strategy that produces successful results

Note: This is not a word-for-word transcript.

Find an A-Z list of our programmes.

Learn more about jargon in this Business jargon podcast series.

Practise your reading skills with The Reading Room.

Have you ever received an uncivil email?

What foods did you dislike when you were growing up?

Children are spending too much time with electronic devices.

When was the last time you bought something you didn't need?

## 高频词汇学习

- **fussy** /ˈfʌsi/
  - 词性：adj.
  - 中文释义：挑剔的，过分讲究的
  - 原文例句：Are you a fussy eater?
  - 搭配用法：fussy about sth（对某事挑剔）；fussy eater（挑食的人）

- **encourage** /ɪnˈkʌrɪdʒ/
  - 词性：v.
  - 中文释义：鼓励，激励
  - 原文例句：But what can parents do to help encourage their children to eat a healthy diet?
  - 搭配用法：encourage sb to do sth（鼓励某人做某事）；encourage growth（促进增长）

- **disaster** /dɪˈzɑːstə/
  - 词性：n.
  - 中文释义：灾难，灾祸
  - 原文例句：...without turning every dinner into disaster?
  - 搭配用法：a disaster（一场灾难）；disaster area（灾区）

- **nutritionist** /njuːˈtrɪʃənɪst/
  - 词性：n.
  - 中文释义：营养学家
  - 原文例句：Which of the following does the child nutritionist Charlotte Stirling-Reed recommend for stress-free family meals?
  - 搭配用法：child nutritionist（儿童营养学家）；consult a nutritionist（咨询营养学家）

- **pressure** /ˈpreʃə/
  - 词性：v./n.
  - 中文释义：对……施加压力；压力
  - 原文例句：don't pressure your child to eat everything on the plate
  - 搭配用法：pressure sb to do sth（强迫某人做某事）；under pressure（在压力下）

- **flavour** /ˈfleɪvə/
  - 词性：n.
  - 中文释义：味道，风味
  - 原文例句：introduce your child to lots of new flavours quickly
  - 搭配用法：new flavours（新口味）；flavour of sth（……的味道）

- **battleground** /ˈbætlɡraʊnd/
  - 词性：n.
  - 中文释义：战场；争论的焦点
  - 原文例句：place where argument or conflict happens
  - 搭配用法：political battleground（政治战场）；become a battleground（成为战场）

- **mood** /muːd/
  - 词性：n.
  - 中文释义：心情，情绪
  - 原文例句：how a person is feeling at a particular time
  - 搭配用法：in a good/bad mood（心情好/坏）；mood swings（情绪波动）

- **winning formula** /ˈwɪnɪŋ ˈfɔːmjʊlə/
  - 词性：短语
  - 中文释义：成功的秘诀，制胜法宝
  - 原文例句：a method or strategy that produces successful results
  - 搭配用法：have a winning formula（有成功的秘诀）；discover the winning formula（发现制胜法宝）

- **allergy** /ˈælədʒi/
  - 词性：n.
  - 中文释义：过敏，过敏反应
  - 原文例句：medical condition where certain foods make your body sick
  - 搭配用法：food allergy（食物过敏）；have an allergy to sth（对某物过敏）

## 阅读理解练习

1. 根据文章，许多人对童年时的食物有什么共同记忆？
   A. 他们一直都很喜欢那些食物
   B. 他们曾经讨厌某些食物，有些人至今仍讨厌
   C. 他们从不挑食
   D. 他们只吃水果和蔬菜

   答案：B

2. 文章主要讨论了什么问题？
   A. 如何烹饪健康的晚餐
   B. 父母如何鼓励孩子健康饮食而不让吃饭变成灾难
   C. 孩子们最喜欢的食物是什么
   D. 营养学家的工作内容

   答案：B

3. 儿童营养学家Charlotte Stirling-Reed建议什么来实现无压力的家庭用餐？
   A. 确保孩子每天吃五份水果和蔬菜
   B. 不要强迫孩子吃完盘子里的所有食物
   C. 快速让孩子接触许多新口味
   D. 以上都不是（文章未直接给出答案，需通过听力获取）

   答案：D

4. 以下哪个词汇的意思是“一种能产生成功结果的方法或策略”？
   A. battleground
   B. mood
   C. winning formula
   D. food allergy

   答案：C

5. 根据文章，“ups and downs”指的是什么？
   A. 上下楼梯
   B. 好与坏的事情交织发生
   C. 情绪波动
   D. 食物的味道变化

   答案：B

## 今日学习要点

**语法要点：**
- **used to do sth**：表示过去常常做某事，但现在不再做了。例如：“foods that we used to hate as children”（我们小时候曾经讨厌的食物）。注意“used to”后接动词原形，强调过去的状态或习惯。
- **without + 动名词**：表示“在没有……的情况下”。例如：“without turning every dinner into disaster”（不让每顿晚餐变成灾难）。这是常见的介词后接动名词的结构。

**表达技巧：**
- **a fussy eater**：这是一个非常地道的表达，意思是“挑食的人”。在日常对话中，可以用“picky eater”作为同义替换。
- **a big deal**：表示“重要的事情”。常用于否定句或疑问句中，如“It's not a big deal”（没什么大不了的）。
- **winning formula**：这是一个习语，字面意思是“获胜的配方”，实际指“成功的秘诀”。在商务或日常讨论中都可以使用。

**文化背景：**
- 本文讨论了儿童挑食这一普遍现象。在西方文化中，父母通常鼓励孩子尝试新食物，但现代营养学强调避免在餐桌上制造压力，以免孩子产生负面情绪。Charlotte Stirling-Reed是英国著名的儿童营养学家，她的建议常强调“无压力用餐”（stress-free family meals），即不要强迫孩子吃饭，而是通过正面引导培养健康的饮食习惯。这与许多亚洲家庭中“必须吃完碗里所有食物”的传统观念有所不同。
